meaning: n.故弄玄虚的言词,欺人之谈
examples: That 20th-century connoisseur of doublespeak, George Orwell, would not have been surprised.
They noted the lack of outcry when a columnist declared that, for Catholics, "doublespeak and duplicity are second nature".
It also explores doublespeak in terms of rhetorical devices, namely, personification, dehumanization, metaphor, understatement and inflation.
He fired back at McCain, accusing his rival of a little doublespeak of his own.
Such dithering and doublespeak is reminiscent of the hidebound socialist past that India began to discard in 1991.
